dashboard.title: Dashboard
dashboard.noLogs: Logs will appear here once the backend is connected.
chat.title: Chat
chat.noConversationsHint: Don't be shy — your agent is waiting for you. Start chatting and discover what it can do for you
chat.typeMessageHint: Type a message... (Ctrl/Cmd + Enter to send)
chat.selectConversation: Select a conversation to start chatting
chat.deleteUser.title: Delete user and all their data
chat.deleteUser.description: This action is irreversible. The following data will be permanently deleted:
chat.deleteUser.item.permissions: Tool permissions assigned to this user
chat.deleteUser.confirmLabel: To confirm, type the user's name:
tasks.title: Tasks
tasks.scheduleHint: Cron expression (cyclic) or ISO 8601 datetime (one-shot). Empty = run immediately.
tasks.scheduleHintOneShot: ISO 8601 datetime, e.g. "2026-04-01T09:00:00Z". Empty = run immediately.
tasks.promptPlaceholder: Enter the task prompt or instructions
tasks.noTasksHint: Create a task to automate recurring actions or one-shot jobs for the agent
skills.capabilitiesHint: Review the capabilities available to the agent
memory.title: Memory
memory.selectToView: Select an entry to view details
memory.label: Label
memory.noProperties: No properties. Click "Add property" to begin.
memory.deleteConfirm: Are you sure you want to delete {{name}}?
memory.noMemoryHint: Start a conversation with the agent — it will automatically build its memory as it learns about you
mcps.title: Tools
mcps.builtinHint: These are native capabilities embedded in the agent. Availability is controlled from the server config. User access to the LLM is managed in the Permissions tab.
mcps.cap.browserDesc: Allows the agent to browse and fetch web page content.
mcps.cap.terminalDesc: Allows the agent to execute shell commands in a secure terminal.
mcps.cap.subagentsDesc: Allows the agent to launch and orchestrate subagent instances.
mcps.cap.memoryDesc: Allows the agent to read and write to the long-term memory graph.
mcps.cap.filesystemDesc: Allows the agent to read and write files directly on the server.
mcps.cap.sessionsDesc: Allows the agent to interact with and inspect other active agent sessions.
mcps.cap.mcpDesc: Allows the agent to call tools exposed by connected MCP servers.
mcps.cap.audioDesc: Enables audio capabilities of the underlying model (voice, ASR, TTS).
mcps.noServersHint: Connect a Model Context Protocol server to extend the agent's capabilities with new tools
permissions.title: Permissions
permissions.selectUser: Select a user to manage their tool permissions
permissions.noUsersHint: Users appear here once their pairing with the agent has been approved
permissions.noToolsHint: Connect an MCP server to view available tools
permissions.defaultDeny: Default policy: allow. Explicitly denied tools will not be accessible to the model.
mcps.httpOnlyNote: For security reasons only Streamable HTTP transport is supported. To use stdio-based MCPs, connect them via a bridge such as mcp-proxy.
mcps.apiKeyPlaceholder: Enter the API key if required
mcps.availableTools: List of available tools from this server
mcps.oauthPending: Authorization pending — complete it in the popup
skills.title: Skills
skills.enabled: Enabled capabilities are injected as tools into the agent's LLM prompt context.
skills.noSkillsHint: Place skill files in the workspace/skills folder and restart the agent
settings.title: Settings
settings.systemPromptDesc: Instructions that define the agent's behavior
settings.loadingHint: Fetching your agent settings from the server
settings.loadError: Failed to load configuration from server
settings.saveInvalidResponse: Invalid response from server while saving configuration
settings.field.themeDesc: Interface color scheme. Stored locally in the browser.
settings.field.agentNameDesc: Display name for this agent instance
settings.field.providerDesc: AI provider used for agent tasks
settings.field.modelDesc: Choose the default model for agent tasks
settings.field.apiKeyDesc: API key for the selected provider
settings.field.baseURLDesc: Base URL for the OpenAI-compatible provider API (e.g. http://localhost:8000/v1)
settings.field.ollamaApiKeyDesc: Optional API key for protected or remote Ollama instances
settings.field.dockerModelRunnerEndpoint: Docker Model Runner API endpoint (default: http://localhost:12434/engines/v1)
settings.field.dockerModelRunnerModelDes: Model name to use with Docker Model Runner (e.g. ai/smollm2)
settings.field.capabilitiesDesc: Enable or disable core agent features such as browser automation, terminal commands, subagents, memory, MCP integration, filesystem access, and session interaction.
settings.field.capabilities_audioDesc: Enable audio features of the underlying model (voice, TTS, STT)
settings.field.capabilities_filesystemDe: Enable direct file read and write access on the server
settings.field.capabilities_sessionsDesc: Enable interaction with and inspection of other active agent sessions
settings.field.graphqlBaseUrlDesc: Public URL of the server (e.g. https://openlobster.example.com). Used for OAuth callbacks and MCP redirects.
settings.field.secretsBackendDesc: Where agent secrets and credentials are stored
settings.field.schedulerEnabledDesc: Enable the task scheduler event loop
settings.field.schedulerMemoryIntervalDe: How often to run memory consolidation (e.g. "4h")
settings.field.channelDiscordTokenDesc: Bot token from the Discord Developer Portal
settings.field.channelSlackEnabledDesc: Activate the Slack bot channel via Socket Mode
settings.field.channelWhatsAppEnabledDes: Activate the WhatsApp Business API channel
settings.field.channelWhatsAppPhoneIdDes: Phone Number ID from Meta Business Suite
settings.field.channelWhatsAppApiTokenDe: Permanent or temporary access token from Meta Business Suite
settings.field.channelTwilioAccountSidDe: Account SID from the Twilio Console
settings.field.channelTwilioAuthTokenDes: Auth token from the Twilio Console
settings.field.channelTwilioFromNumberDe: Twilio phone number used to send messages (E.164, e.g. +15551234567)
mobile.blocked.title: Desktop Required
mobile.blocked.message: This application requires a desktop browser. Please access it from a computer.
browser.outdated.title: Browser Not Supported
browser.outdated.message: Your browser is out of date. Please upgrade to a modern version for the best experience.
browser.outdated.features: Required: ES6, Proxy, Async/Await, CSS Grid
marketplace.title: MCP Marketplace
marketplace.subtitle: Discover and connect MCP servers with one click
marketplace.error: Error loading marketplace. Check your connection.
marketplace.name: Name
accessToken.title: Access required
accessToken.description1: Enter the access token to continue. You can find it in the application configuration under
pairing.title: Pairing Request
pairing.displayNameHint: How this user will appear in conversations. A new account will be created and linked to this channel automatically.
wizard.welcome.title: Welcome to OpenLobster
wizard.welcome.description: Let's configure your agent in a few simple steps. You can change these settings later.
wizard.agentConfig.title: Agent configuration
wizard.agentConfig.description: Give your agent a name and set the server URL.
wizard.aiProvider.title: AI provider
wizard.aiProvider.description: Choose the AI model and provider for your agent.
wizard.channels.title: Communication channels
wizard.channels.description: Enable the channels where your agent will receive messages (Telegram, Discord, etc.).
wizard.capabilities.title: Agent capabilities
wizard.capabilities.description: Enable or disable the features your agent can use (browser, terminal, memory, etc.).
wizard.marketplace.title: MCP Marketplace
wizard.marketplace.description: Connect MCP servers to extend your agent's capabilities. You can skip this step.
wizard.done.title: All set!
wizard.done.description: Your agent is ready. Click Finish to save and start using OpenLobster.
